sequence.title: Step by Step
sequence.explain: A program runs its blocks one after another, in order — just like following a recipe. Each step waits its turn.

turn.title: Turning
turn.explain: A 'turn' block spins your robot to face a new direction without moving it forward. Mix turns with moves to travel any path.

loop.title: Repeat Blocks
loop.explain: Instead of copying the same block again and again, a 'repeat' block runs everything inside it a set number of times. Fewer blocks, same result!

conditional.title: Making Choices
conditional.explain: An 'if' block checks something first — like whether the path ahead is clear — and only runs its blocks when that's true.

variable.title: Remembering Numbers
variable.explain: A variable is a labeled box that holds a number you can reuse. Set it once, then use it anywhere — change the box, and everything using it updates too.

obstacleAvoidance.title: Watching for Walls
obstacleAvoidance.explain: Check whether the path ahead is clear before moving. If it isn't, turn instead of crashing — planning ahead beats guessing.

nudge.idle: Not sure where to start? Drag a block into the workspace and press Run — you can always try again!
nudge.genericHint: Try changing the order of your blocks, or how many times a loop repeats.
nudge.explainOffer: This level is all about {{concept}}. Want me to explain how it works?
